word_list.word: while two dogs are fighting for bone, a third (one|dog) runs away with it
word_list.pronunciation: wˌaɪl tˈuː dˈɑːɡz ɑːɹ fˈaɪɾɪŋ fɔːɹ bˈoʊn ɐ θˈɜːd wˈʌn ɔːɹ dˈɑːɡ ɹˈʌnz ɐwˈeɪ wɪð ɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that while two people or groups are too busy competing or fighting with each other, a third person or group can take advantage of the situation and benefit from it

word_list.word: burn not your house to fright away the mice
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɜːn nˌɑːt jʊɹ hˈaʊs tə fɹˈaɪt ɐwˈeɪ ðə mˈaɪs

senses.definition: used to imply that it is unwise to use drastic or extreme measures to solve a small problem, as it may cause greater harm than good

examples.x: My mother repeated the proverb 'burn not your house to fright away the mice,' as a reminder to not overreact to minor issues and to find more reasonable solutions.

word_list.word: in vain the net is spread in the sight of the bird
word_list.pronunciation: ɪn vˈeɪn ðə nˈɛt ɪz spɹˈɛd ɪnðə sˈaɪt ʌvðə bˈɜːd

senses.definition: used to suggest that efforts made in plain sight or without strategy are often futile and ineffective

word_list.word: the mouse that has but one hole is (quickly|easily) taken
word_list.pronunciation: ðə mˈaʊs ðæt hɐz bˌʌt wˈʌn hˈoʊl ɪz kwˈɪkli ɔːɹ ˈiːzɪli tˈeɪkən

senses.definition: used to imply that having only one option or plan can be risky and leave a person vulnerable to danger or failure, emphasizing the importance of being resourceful and adaptable

examples.x: The captain of the ship reminded his crew that the mouse that has but one hole is quickly taken, as he instructed them to check all of the ship's safety systems before setting sail.

word_list.word: if you run after two hares, you will catch neither
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː ɹˈʌn ˈæftɚ tˈuː hˈɛɹz juː wɪl kˈætʃ nˈiːðɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that trying to do too many things at once can lead to a lack of focus and a decrease in overall effectiveness

word_list.word: the tongue is but three inches long, yet (it|) can kill a man six feet tall
word_list.pronunciation: ðə tˈʌŋ ɪz bˌʌt θɹˈiː ˈɪntʃᵻz lˈɑːŋ jˈɛt ɪt kæn kˈɪl ɐ mˈæn sˈɪks fˈiːt tˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to highlight the power of words to cause harm or destruction, encouraging individuals to use them thoughtfully and with care for their impact on others

word_list.word: the tongue is not (made of|) steel, yet it cuts
word_list.pronunciation: ðə tˈʌŋ ɪz nˌɑːt mˌeɪd ʌv stˈiːl jˈɛt ɪt kˈʌts

senses.definition: used to suggest that words have the power to hurt or harm others, and that care should be taken to use them wisely and with consideration for their impact

word_list.word: a (joke|jest) never gains an enemy, but often loses a friend
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dʒˈoʊk ɔːɹ dʒˈɛst nˈɛvɚ ɡˈeɪnz ɐn ˈɛnəmi bˌʌt ˈɔfən lˈuːzᵻz ɐ fɹˈɛnd

senses.definition: used to warn that while humor may not create enemies, it can often offend or hurt friends and cause them to distance themselves

word_list.word: gentility without ability is worse than plain beggary
word_list.pronunciation: dʒɛntˈɪlɪɾi wɪðˌaʊt ɐbˈɪləɾi ɪz wˈɜːs ðɐn plˈeɪn bˈɛɡɚɹi

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of valuing competence over social status, as having a high status without the necessary skills can lead to arrogance, incompetence, and ultimately failure

examples.x: The politician had risen to power on the back of his family's name and reputation, but when it came time to lead, he was found wanting - indeed, gentility without ability is worse than plain beggary.

word_list.word: it is an ill bird that (fouls|defiles) its own nest
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ɐn ˈɪl bˈɜːd ðæt fˈaʊlz dɪfˈaɪlz ɪts ˈoʊn nˈɛst

senses.definition: used to advise against harming one's own environment, community, or relationships, as it will ultimately lead to negative consequences for oneself

examples.x: The business owner who mistreated their employees was a reminder that it is an ill bird that fouls its own nest, as their actions ultimately led to high turnover rates and damaged their own business.

word_list.word: the pitcher will go to the well once too often
word_list.pronunciation: ðə pˈɪtʃɚ wɪl ɡˌoʊ tə ðə wˈɛl wˈʌns tˈuː ˈɔfən

senses.definition: used to warn against overusing a particular approach or strategy, as doing so can lead to diminishing returns or even failure

examples.x: He kept borrowing money without repaying it, but the pitcher will go to the well once too often, and eventually, his friends stopped lending him money.

word_list.word: a cat in gloves catches no mice
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ kˈæt ɪn ɡlˈʌvz kˈætʃᵻz nˈoʊ mˈaɪs

senses.definition: used to imply that being overly cautious or passive prevents one from taking action and achieving desired results

examples.x: My grandfather used to say that a cat in gloves catches no mice, to remind me that sometimes you need to take risks in order to achieve success.
